title: Keep prompt assembly deterministic
description: The same inputs always produce the same prompt; no clock, random ordering, or unstable iteration leaks into assembly.
body: Use when building the code that constructs a prompt from parts. Do not interpolate timestamps, random ids, or map-iteration-order-dependent fields into the prompt, and avoid nondeterministic formatting. Actions: sort keys and lists deterministically, freeze the template, isolate any genuinely dynamic value behind an explicit parameter, and snapshot the assembled prompt for given inputs. Validation signals include a test that the same inputs yield a byte-identical prompt across runs. Risk boundary: nondeterministic assembly makes evaluation and debugging unreproducible and can hide regressions; treat it as a defect, not a stylistic choice.
